meaning: vi.茂盛的生长,生活奢侈,沉溺
examples: We can luxuriate for a change .
They nonchalantly dived in, swam swiftly across the pool and pulled themselves up on the big rocks to luxuriate the sun.
Once upon a time, Portuguese royals and aristocrats would retreat to the rippling hills of Sintra and luxuriate in its Eden-like gardens.
You go to luxuriate in its old-school resorts and daydream on its un-peopled beaches.
You must never skim a novel, because you're not reading a novel for information, you're reading it to luxuriate in those Updike sentences.
